title: Compare Fraction Sizes with Same Whole
description: Determine which shaded amount is larger when the same whole is partitioned into different numbers of equal pieces.
condition: Two representations of the same whole are divided into different numbers of equal parts with one part shaded in each.
response: Conclude that the whole divided into fewer equal parts has larger pieces, so one half is greater than one quarter.
label: Principle / Model
example: If one square shows $\frac{1}{2}$ shaded and another shows $\frac{1}{4}$ shaded, then $\frac{1}{2} > \frac{1}{4}$ because the same whole is divided into fewer equal parts.

title: Draw Shape on Dot Grid
description: Construct a specified geometric shape using dots as vertices and straight line segments.
condition: Prompt to draw a named shape (e.g., square, rectangle, triangle) on a dot grid.
response: Connect appropriate dots with straight segments to form the named shape.
label: Production / Skill
example: Draw a square by connecting four dots to form four equal sides and four right angles.

title: Identify Triangle by 3 Straight Sides
description: Recognize a triangle as a closed shape with exactly three straight sides and three vertices.
condition: A presented shape with visible sides and vertices.
response: Classify the shape as a triangle if it has exactly three straight sides and is closed.
label: Concept
example: A shape with 3 straight sides and 3 corners is identified as a triangle.

title: Label Shape with Given Letter
description: Write the specified letter to label a drawn shape.
condition: Instruction to label a drawn shape with a specific given letter.
response: Write the provided letter on or near the shape.
label: Association
example: After drawing a triangle, write "T" next to it.
notes: Letter varies by prompt but each instance is a fixed association.

title: Read Time on Analog Clock
description: Interpret the positions of the hour and minute hands to determine the time shown.
condition: An analog clock with hour and minute hands at specific positions.
response: State the corresponding time in hours and minutes.
label: Principle / Model
example: If the minute hand points to 6 and the hour hand is between 4 and 5, the time is 4:30.

title: Recognize O’Clock Time
description: Identify times where the minute hand points to 12 as o’clock times.
condition: An analog clock with the minute hand pointing to 12.
response: State that the time is exactly an o’clock time (e.g., 5:00).
label: Principle / Model
example: If the minute hand points to 12 and the hour hand points to 5, the time is 5:00.

title: Shade Fraction of Shape
description: Shade a specified fractional part of a shape by partitioning it into equal parts and shading the required number.
condition: Instruction to shade a given fraction (e.g., one half, one quarter) of a shape.
response: Partition the shape into equal parts and shade the number of parts corresponding to the fraction.
label: Principle / Model
example: To shade $\frac{1}{4}$ of a square, divide it into 4 equal parts and shade 1 part.
